participants: FILL_IN — list everyone in the meeting besides the speaker

context_window.raw_content: FILL_IN — paste 8-10 lines of surrounding context from the transcript

signal.raw_content: FILL_IN — paste the specific 1-3 line excerpt containing the signal. Must be a substring of context_window.raw_content.
ambiguity_flags: FILL_IN — note any pronoun references, implicit owners, or context needed to interpret the signal. Delete this array entry if none.

attribute_updates.project_reasoning: FILL_IN — why this project and not another. If using external knowledge, flag it: 'From external [type] context (not in transcript): [fact] (source: [source]).'
attribute_updates.primary_attribute: FILL_IN — blocker | risk | decision | task | milestone | lessons_learned
attribute_updates.primary_attribute_reasoning: FILL_IN — why this attribute type and not another. Cite specific language from the signal.

sub_attribute_updates.reasoning: FILL_IN — how was owner identified? (explicit name, speaker/listener inference, role mapping). Flag external knowledge if used.

sub_attribute_updates.value: FILL_IN — see ground_truth_schema.json for allowed values per attribute type

sub_attribute_updates.reasoning: FILL_IN — why this priority level? Flag external knowledge if used.

attribute_updates._note: Sub-attribute fields vary by primary_attribute type. See ground_truth_schema.json for the exact fields. The example above is for a task. For blocker use: summary, severity, owner, status, blocking_since. For risk use: summary, severity, likelihood, status, owner, mitigation_plan. For decision use: summary, made_by, made_at, impact, rationale. For milestone use: name, status, due_date, completion_date. For lessons_learned: remove sub_attribute_updates and use top-level value + value_reasoning instead.
